posts.text: An oil windfall and investment returns have doubled the size of one country’s wealth fund over the past decade. This is having perverse consequences. To learn what they are, read on by registering for free https://www.economist.com/finance-and-economics/2026/04/01/can-a-country-get-too-rich?taid=69d114b7f0c12100012b98d3&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
BREAKING: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi says Iran's position on peace talks in Pakistan is being "misrepresented by US media." 

"We are deeply grateful to Pakistan for its efforts and have never refused to go to Islamabad. What we care about are the terms of a conclusive and lasting END to the illegal war that is imposed on us," he says
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text: Farmer question:  Trying to open up the branch structure of an orange tree (pic 1). Trying bricks with twine as they allow me to adjust over time. Why is this better/worse than the stakes I used on my jackfruit (pic 2)?? Stakes are VERY sturdy, but cannot easily reposition. Thoughts??
posts.author: hkuppy
